title: Security Parameter Drift on In-Scope Routers from Gold Config Hash

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 2.2.3 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 2.2.3 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.11.13: Security Parameter Drift on In-Scope Routers from Gold Config Hash.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.13' running on index=compliance sourcetype="compliance:network_config_digest" ‚Äî device, config_hash, baseline_hash, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=compliance sourcetype="compliance:network_config_digest" earliest=-48h
| lookup pci_perimeter_devices.csv device OUTPUT pci_tier
| where pci_tier IN ("cde_border","dmz")
| eval drift=if(config_hash!=baseline_hash,1,0)
| where drift=1
| stats latest(_time) as last_drift by device, config_hash, baseline_hash
| sort device
description: Compares periodic configuration snapshots to an approved gold hash per device so unintended drift in security-relevant parameters is detected between formal audits.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes compliance and sourcetypes compliance:network_config_digest. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Nightly job pushes SHA-256 of normalized running-config. (2) Store `baseline_hash` only after CAB approval. (3) Auto-create incident on drift. (4) Retain hash history 13 months for PCI logging retention alignment. Indexes required: `index=compliance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=compliance:network_config_digest`; Lookups: `pci_perimeter_devices`, `pci_perimeter_devices.csv`.
visualization: Table (drifted devices), sparkline if hashes trend over time in summary index, single value (drift count).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="compliance:network_config_digest"` ‚Äî `device`, `config_hash`, `baseline_hash`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Nightly job pushes SHA-256 of normalized running-config. (2) Store `baseline_hash` only after CAB approval. (3) Auto-create incident on drift. (4) Retain hash history 13 months for PCI logging retention alignment.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="compliance:network_config_digest" earliest=-48h
| lookup pci_perimeter_devices.csv device OUTPUT pci_tier
| where pci_tier IN ("cde_border","dmz")
| eval drift=if(config_hash!=baseline_hash,1,0)
| where drift=1
| stats latest(_time) as last_drift by device, config_hash, baseline_hash
| sort device
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Parameter Drift on In-Scope Routers from Gold Config Hash (PCI DSS Req 2.2.1, 2.2.3)** ‚Äî Compares periodic configuration snapshots to an approved gold hash per device so unintended drift in security-relevant parameters is detected between formal audits.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="compliance:network_config_digest"` ‚Äî `device`, `config_hash`, `baseline_hash`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance; **sourcetype**: compliance:network_config_digest. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=compliance, sourcetype="compliance:network_config_digest", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where pci_tier IN ("cde_border","dmz")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **drift** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where drift=1` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by device, config_hash, baseline_hash** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Security Parameter Drift on In-Scope Routers from Gold Config Hash (PCI DSS Req 2.2.1, 2.2.3)** ‚Äî Compares periodic configuration snapshots to an approved gold hash per device so unintended drift in security-relevant parameters is detected between formal audits.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="compliance:network_config_digest"` ‚Äî `device`, `config_hash`, `baseline_hash`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (drifted devices), sparkline if hashes trend over time in summary index, single value (drift count).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for compares periodic configuration snapshots to an approved gold hash per device so unintended drift in security-relevant parameters is detected between formal audits. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
